title: cormophyllum capense
intro: Cormophyllum capense is a plant species native to South Africa, belonging to the family Apocynaceae, and is also known as the 'cancer bush' due to its traditional use in treating cancer. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, including iridoids and alkaloids, which have been found to exhibit anti-cancer and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Cormophyllum capense include its potential to inhibit the growth of cancer cells, reduce inflammation, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant is traditionally prepared as a tea or infused in hot water, with some herbalists also recommending the use of tinctures or extracts for medicinal purposes.
